methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rainmaker is a Series A stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $25M to date. The broader comp universe consists of eight private peers in Drones & Autonomous, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method given data constraints. Using total raised ($25M) as a proxy for pre-money value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $50M. The peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints within the sector—only one adjacent-stage comparable was identified against a threshold of three required—and broadening to all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable noise.

The triangulated valuation range is $42M to $57M, with a midpoint of $50M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty. The absence of tight peer comparables means the stage-progression step-up multiplier of 2.0x remains the primary valuation driver and is not independently validated. Key uncertainties include market reception of Rainmaker's specific autonomous platform relative to the heterogeneous peer set and whether the 2.0x progression holds across the current capital environment. A narrow comp set and reliance on one method argue for heightened diligence on company-specific unit economics and go-to-market traction before final pricing.
